payload.answer: The proposed 500 one-row memory cards should not be committed as-is. Please choose how to handle this large table-like input.
repair_options: Do not save any of the 500 proposed row-level memory cards.
Save only a concise summary of the table, if the user confirms what should be remembered.
Ask the user which specific rows or facts are important enough to remember durably.
Save the CSV/table as an external document or reference, and remember only its location or purpose if the user wants that.
Group related rows into a smaller number of thematic memory entries instead of one memory per row.
If the rows represent contacts, preferences, tasks, or records, ask the user to identify the intended entity and retention purpose before saving.
Keep the information in the current conversation only and do not add it to long-term memory.
